AGENT'S NOTES: [REDACTED] showed extreme sincerity throughout the
MEMORANDUM FOR THE OFFICER IN CHARGE:
AAF Base Unit, Ferry Division, Brooks Field, San Antonio, Texas was
interviewed and the following information was obtained: [REDACTED]
departed Williams Field, Arizona at 1400 CST on 28 June 1947 in a P-51
for Portland, Oregon, by the way of Medford, Oregon. At approximately
1515 CST on a course of 300 degrees, and ground speed of 285, altitude
10,000 feet, approximately thirty miles northwest of Lake Meade, Nevada
[REDACTED] sighted five or six white, circular objects at four o'clock,
altitude approximately 6,000 feet, course approximately 120 degrees and
an estimated speed of 285 MPH. [REDACTED] said the objects were flying
very smoothly and in a close formation. The estimated size of the white
objects were approximately 36 inches in diameter. [REDACTED] stated
that he is sure the white objects were not birds, since the rate of
closure was very fast. Lt. Armstrong was certain that the white objects
were not jets or conventional type aircraft since he has flown both types.
[REDACTED] was very sincere in his explanation and was not the
exaggerating type. He merely stated what he saw and has drawn no con-
clusions as to what the white objects were.
Lawrence R. King, Jr., Special Agent, CIC, ADG

AGENT'S NOTES: [REDACTED] is a man of 32 years of age, being married and
a very good provider for his family. [REDACTED] has recently purchased a
attached exhibit. It is the personal opinion of the interviewer that [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] actually saw what he stated that he saw. It is difficult to believe
that a man of [REDACTED] character and apparent integrity would state
did not see them. To go further, if [REDACTED] can write a report of the
saw, it is the opinion of the interviewer that [REDACTED] is in the wrong
business, that he should be writing Buck Rogers fiction. [REDACTED] is
made by [REDACTED] in this report would make it a voluminous volume. However,
after having checked an aeronautical map of the area over which [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] in regard to the distances involved, speed of the objects, course
distances mentioned by [REDACTED] in his report are within a short distance
of the actual distances on aeronautical charts of this area, although [REDACTED]
[REDACTED] has never consulted aeronautical charts of the type the Army uses.
